_readme: Manually-maintained indicators. Each entry: raw (source values consumed by domain/anchors.js), asOf, source, confidence (verified|provisional|judgment), _instructions, nextUpdate. Anchor definitions live in research/anchor-tables.md.

hendrycksAgiScore.source: Hendrycks et al., A Definition of AGI (arXiv:2510.18212, agidefinition.ai)
hendrycksAgiScore._instructions: Update when the consortium publishes a score for a new frontier model. Use their number verbatim; do not interpolate between models.

epochBenchmarks._instructions: TEMPORARY. Replace with automated Epoch adapter once basket B-2026.1 members are confirmed against what Epoch serves (open challenge 2, anchor-tables.md). Until then this is a judgment placeholder — mean fraction-of-expert-human across {GPQA Diamond, SWE-bench Verified, FrontierMath, HLE}.

arcGap.source: ARC Prize technical reports (arXiv:2601.10904, arXiv:2603.24621); verify current at arcprize.org
arcGap._instructions: Update from arcprize.org leaderboards quarterly. frontierOverHuman = frontier score / human score per active generation. Generations retire at ratio > 0.85 (ratchet rule) — log retirement in decision log. NOTE: an unverified secondary claim puts ARC-AGI-2 much higher (97.9% by a meta-system, Apr 2026) — verify before next publish.

selfLearning.source: CL-Bench (arXiv:2606.05661) — Gain barely positive across domains
selfLearning._instructions: gainRatio = observed Gain as fraction of human learning-curve improvement. Human baselines not yet published by CL-Bench, so this is judgment-tagged. Re-estimate when CL-Bench or successors publish comparable human curves.

realTimeEngagement.source: FutureWatch rubric D2 (research/anchor-tables.md); evidence: public voice-mode deployments (M1), group-chat demos (M2 partial)
realTimeEngagement._instructions: Quarterly scoring session. Each milestone 0/10/20 with written justification appended to scoringLog. Evidence standard: public demonstrations or peer-reviewed evals, not vendor marketing. Vaughan signs off.

scoringLog.note: Initial scoring at rubric approval. M1: real-time voice with interruption handling is deployed at consumer scale. M2: multi-party tracking shown in demos, not robust in the wild. M3-M5: no credible public evidence.

agenticAutonomyLevel.source: Morris et al. autonomy rubric (arXiv:2311.02462), scored on routine deployment
agenticAutonomyLevel._instructions: Quarterly. routineLevel ∈ tool|consultant|collaborator|expert|agent = highest level ROUTINE in deployed general-purpose systems. nextDemonstrated = next level credibly shown in limited settings (+10).

anthropicEconIndex._instructions: Update per AEI report (~quarterly). breadth = fraction of occupational categories with material AI task usage; depth = AI share of TOTAL work performed (NOT automation share of AI traffic — see anchor-tables.md challenge note). Both require reading the report's underlying data, not the headline stats. Flag: proxy for the economy via Claude usage; cross-check against aiIndexEconomy.

aiIndexEconomy.source: Stanford HAI AI Index 2026, Economy chapter (hai.stanford.edu/ai-index)
aiIndexEconomy._instructions: Annual (~April). adoption01 = firm genAI adoption rebased 2019=0, near-universal=1. postings01 = AI share of job postings rebased likewise. Weakest anchor set in the system — candidate for replacement if a better public series appears.

friLeapAgi.source: Forecasting Research Institute — LEAP panel, Wave 8 (forecastingresearch.org); cf. Samotsvety Forecasting (~28% probability of AGI by 2030)
friLeapAgi._instructions: Replaces the old Metaculus-API expectation feed (Metaculus stopped serving live aggregation data via API, 2026-07, anti-abuse policy — see decision log D4). LEAP surveys AI experts and superforecasters monthly; medianYear is conditional on AGI occurring, using LEAP's definition (commercially available system beating 90th-percentile professionals on 90% of economically useful non-physical tasks at <=5x human labor cost — NOT the same definition as metrTimeHorizon, do not conflate). Check forecastingresearch.substack.com each wave; update on a >=1yr shift in either median, quarterly review minimum.

fliSafetyIndex._instructions: Semiannual (summer/winter). existentialGrade = best lab's existential-safety domain grade — this drives the divergence bar (harsher-grade decision, anchor-tables.md). overallBestGpa recorded for context display only.
